reviews.review_msg: had for 321 and 426. Grant is one of my favourite professors at SFU. He explains everything really easily, and engages the class really well. He is very straightforward regarding expectations and is genuinely a very nice and relaxed guy.

Chill guy. Great at simplifying concepts + always ready to answer your questions.

1 midterm and 1 final (both non cumulative); rest of grades is online assignments.

Overall, can't complain, he's a really good professor.

Tests are fair; combination of theory and calculations.
Literally does not provide any accommodations, worst prof! Avoid!!

Taking him for 321. He is a great professor, the workload is quite light (midterm, final, and weekly assignments that take less than half an hour). He explains everything in simple terms, and provides practice problems that are similar to exam questions. Definetely recommend!
Preparation is key!

michael is an amazing prof. I can understand why people say hes hard but I really enjoy the real-life stories he shares and I found real value with the assignments we did in class especially with technology playing a bigger role now than ever before. Overall, I feel that I truly learnt something from his class. would take his classes again

He is a very mean professor. He talked so fast in class which you will not have time to take any notes. He did not give out any powerpoint slide before and most importantly you have to read the textbook. He wont give any half/ partial marks if your answer make sense but no quite really the sample answer. Not caring professor.

so easy to get distracted in lecture, prof spend half an hour talking thing that doesn't relate to class lol. be prepared to read every single word in the book and understand concepts in the corner of the book. exams grading is ridiculous... even try hard to explain a question, while, you have single variation with the "right" opinion, you get 0

Praise is an engaging prof! She always tries to go above and beyond to create fun lectures. The content is the difficult part. There is a lot to memorize and the audit process is quite complicated. As such, the prof and (dry) textbook are your best friends. Exams are reasonable; MC are a bit tricky, long answers very similar to in-class activities.

Hands down the best professor I've ever had at SFU. Explained concepts well during lectures and went the extra mile with slides and in-class activities to facilitate engagement (Kahoot, in-class problems). If you take BUS 426 (Audit), I highly recommend taking her class.
He is a pretty good professor, know what he is talking about. BUT, make sure you do read the text book and his notes...

Terrible lecturer, just repeats everything in the text. Exams are not really fair as he tests u on auditing applications, which he doesn't cover much. You'll have to do a lot of self-studying for this course.
Good prof and good class, but be sure to bring something along to lectures to keep yourself awake.
Not for ESL students.

Very ambiguous way of teaching. Asks you very obscure questions on auditing and then penalize you for not having the same answers that he had though. Multiple choice questions are ill prepared and lack objectivity. Overall not a good teacher.
Did not like his teaching at all. Only This guy has the ability to take a relatively easy course and make it exorbitantly hard to pass.

The most subjective marker I have ever had at SFU. Will give you a grade based on what mood he's in that day. Assignment/group project criteria is unclear, and he will give you a mark on the group project without telling you why you got that mark. If you have experience in audit/accounting, you'll do well cause the exam Qs are open-ended.

Grading criteria isn't clear at all. Multiple people from my class had emailed him weeks in advance to the final and he provided no clarification whatsoever. No practice problems were assigned throughout the semester, and he refused to provide any when asked. This made studying for the final even more difficult due to practical inexperience.
Passionate about course, overall a nice guy
He is not a good professor. He is vague about the grading skeeme. Avoid him.
He does not give clear guidelines as to what to expect in the exam. Overall not a good prof.

Dana is an expert in the field of auditing. He is very approachable and cares about all his students. He does an excellent job helping students learn about the basic framework and principles of auditing. Midterm (memorization) and final (application) were different.  Would be helpful if he used a mic. And written report requirements were unclear.

Professor Grieg is a very knowledgeable and passionate  individual about the subjects he teaches. With years of real world business experience he was able to bring the subject to life and give unique scenarios/cases of how the course was relevant. As a class of future CPA's we were all able to gain a healthy respect for being ethical & professional

He's a very good prof and tries to explain everything clearly. What I liked most about his class is that he doesn't give exams based on dead theory/memory-based questions. Instead, he gives you cases and you are expected to answer accordingly. Very useful if you plan on taking CASB. One of the best in SFU!
